name: Buckler (Hai)
description: Built upon a Dagger chassis, the Buckler plays a very different combat role from its smaller sibling. Lacking any weapon mounts, it features a high number of shield emitters and some of the toughest hull armor of any interceptor in the galaxy. 	Buckler pilots are expected to prioritize the defense of the flagship they are meant to protect over their own safety. To encourage this, new Bucklers purchased from Lionheart include a free training video installed on the ship's computer. Mixed in with tutorials for operating and maintaining the spacecraft are inspirational stories praising heroic pilots who sacrificed themselves for the greater good.

name: Class C Freighter (Bulk Freighter (Biohazard))
description: The Class C "Freighter" is a Q-ship, a stealth warship designed by the Syndicate for baiting pirates with the offer of what appears to be a helpless, ordinary Bulk Freighter. 	Inside, a layer of Interference Plating hides its military-grade equipment and a dozen combat drones secreted away in its converted cargo holds, while its faux gearing conceals extra shield emitters.

name: Class C Freighter (Bulk Freighter (Secret Cargo) )
description: The Class C "Freighter" is a Q-ship, a stealth warship designed by the Syndicate for baiting pirates with the offer of what appears to be a helpless, ordinary Bulk Freighter. 	Inside, a layer of Interference Plating hides its military-grade equipment and a dozen combat drones secreted away in its converted cargo holds, while its faux gearing conceals extra shield emitters.

name: Bulk Freighter (hai drones)
description: The Bulk Freighter is the ultimate cargo ship, able to carry as much cargo as four ordinary Syndicate Freighters. Its on-board gearing allows it to service even the most primitive colonies, though dock workers in busier spaceports often joke that it would be simpler to carry smaller freighters within its gigantic holds. 	It is also an incredibly slow and difficult to maneuver ship, especially when loaded down with cargo. Some freight caravans use ordinary freighters instead of Bulk Freighters just to avoid the hassle of constantly having to wait for the Bulk Freighter to properly align itself for hyperspace travel.

name: Carrier (Coalition)
description: The Carrier is more than just a warship: it is practically a mobile space station, with docking bays for an entire squadron of drones and fighters and enough crew to fill a small village. Although designed primarily for engaging enemies from a distance, a Carrier can also do significant damage in close combat.

name: Clipper (Flamethrower)
description: The Clipper is the smaller sister ship to the popular Southbound Shipyards Argosy. Although not able to carry as much cargo, Clippers are versatile ships that can be used as passenger transports, couriers, or even warships in a pinch, and they come with enough free space to be customized based on an individual captain's needs.

name: Combat Drone (Turbo Blaster)
description: Combat drones are pilotless attack ships used primarily by the Republic Navy. Although very weak and easy to destroy, they can be very effective in large numbers. Because drones do not need a cockpit, they can be filled entirely with equipment and solid metal, which makes their hulls stronger than other small ships. 	Drones do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a drone unless you have a ship in your fleet with a drone bay.

name: Container Transport (Billboard TMBR)
description: The Container Transport is a newer, more popular configuration of the Bulk Freighter that replaces the outboard loose cargo holds with intermodal containers for more efficient turnaround in larger, better-equipped ports. 	As with its geared cousin, however, it remains a slow and difficult to maneuver ship, especially when loaded down with cargo. Some freight caravans use ordinary freighters instead of Container Transports just to avoid the hassle of constantly having to wait for the Container Transport to properly align itself for hyperspace travel.

name: Corvette (Inferno Rockets)
description: Incorporating new, experimental lightweight materials, the Lionheart Corvette combines the speed and agility of a fighter with the armament of a heavy warship. Its weak hull is more than compensated for by its advanced shielding matrix.

name: Corvette (Twin Torpedo Pod)
description: Incorporating new, experimental lightweight materials, the Lionheart Corvette combines the speed and agility of a fighter with the armament of a heavy warship. Its weak hull is more than compensated for by its advanced shielding matrix.

name: Cruiser (Coalition)
description: The Republic Cruiser is a relatively recent design, a much larger version of the Frigate designed to be able to take on an entire pirate fleet if necessary. In addition to a wide variety of weaponry, Cruisers have space to carry four attack drones: fast, pilotless fighters that can be controlled remotely.

name: Dagger (Cudgel)
description: The Dagger is a fighter designed by Lionheart Industries, intended to be carried by their Aerie warship, although other ships have been known to carry them, as well. As with many Lionheart ships, its chassis is largely composed of lightweight composite materials, making it considerably faster than other fighters. 	Fighters do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a fighter unless you have a ship in your fleet with a fighter bay.

name: Dreadnought (Flamethrower)
description: The Dreadnought is a new warship that was designed by Southbound Shipyards in order to provide a large capital ship for the Free Worlds fleets, something capable of standing up to a Navy Cruiser or even a Carrier.

name: Dropship (Troops)
description: Dropships were designed with the Navy's Auxiliary in mind. Landed Auxiliaries often act as forward operating bases, so troops needing transport to another area often rely on their Dropships. This cramped and uncomfortable vessel is able to move a squad of Republic soldiers at great speed for its size. 	Fighters do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a fighter unless you have a ship in your fleet with a fighter bay.

name: Enforcer (Modified Blaster)
description: Prior to the formation of the galactic Republic, many star systems were owned by "governing corporate entities," with the vast majority of space being inaccessible to private citizens. The only exception to this rule were the pirate bands, whose few ships were often stolen or rebellious corporate fleets of their own, yet who had an outsized impact on interstellar trade. The necessity for corporations to protect their operations meant that Enforcer was one of the most common vessels used for security and defense. The ship boasts an armament of four gun ports and a turreted mount, all packed on a small but defensible frame that was powerful for its time. 	Although no longer on the market, the Enforcer finds itself in the possession of a small number of merchants and, ironically, various groups of pirates, as an aging combat ship easily outperformed by other light warships or even interceptors of a similar size.

name: Finch (Flamethrower)
description: When the Free Worlds started requesting a Carrier solution from its local shipyards, Tarazed Shipyards quickly saw that a few modifications could make their entry level interceptor into a top tier fighter. Over 85% of the parts that make up the Finch are shared with the Sparrow assembly line, so it's no surprise that even trimmed down to (barely) fit into a fighter bay, the blindingly fast Finch bears strong resemblance to its hyperspace-faring cousin. 	Fighters do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a fighter unless you have a ship in your fleet with a fighter bay.

name: Firebird (Energy Cannons)
description: The Betelgeuse Shipyards Firebird is a warship with a long and storied past; Firebirds fought on both sides of the Alpha Wars over half a millennium ago, and although the equipment and systems have been upgraded continuously since then, the basic design remains almost the same. The Firebird is also a very rugged ship and easy to repair; supposedly a few of the Firebirds that fought in those wars are still in service today.

name: Flivver (Hybrid Afterburner 18)
description: With space for neither cargo nor passengers nor heavy weaponry, the Flivver's only selling point is speed. Flivvers are sold mostly to wealthy individuals who want to be able to travel the stars without needing to share a bunk room on a merchant transport with common people.

name: Frigate (Inferno Rockets)
description: For centuries, the Frigate was the largest warship produced by the Republic Navy Yard, but now they seem quite small compared to a Cruiser or a Carrier. Armed with a variety of weapons, the Frigate is equally capable at fending off small fighters or bombarding larger ships. In its time it possessed relatively good maneuverability for a ship of its size, but now it sees continued use mostly due to the sheer number of them already built for service.

description: For centuries, the Frigate was the largest warship produced by the Republic Navy Yard, but now they seem quite small compared to a Cruiser or a Carrier. Armed with a variety of weapons, the Frigate is equally capable at fending off small fighters or bombarding larger ships. In its time it possessed relatively good maneuverability for a ship of its size, but now it sees continued use mostly due to the sheer number of them already built for service.

name: Freighter (Proton Turret-ATS)
description: The Freighter is designed by the Syndicate to be an efficient means of carrying cargo from system to system, but is so slow and difficult to land that most pilots hate flying them. 	Freighters are an obvious target for pirates, but turn much too slowly to be able to bring any forward-facing guns to bear on an approaching enemy. As a result, the latest model of freighter is equipped only with turrets, including an anti-missile turret to prevent attackers from simply bombarding them into submission from a safe distance.

description: The Fury is Southbound Shipyards' most popular design of escort ship. They have greater firepower than any other interceptor-class vessel, meaning that any pirate flying solo will think twice before attacking a convoy that is accompanied by a Fury. However, Furies are also much less maneuverable than other small ships.

name: Hauler (Energy Cannons)
description: Several centuries ago, in the early days of the Republic, the sudden increase in commerce and space travel led to high demand for cargo ships. Haulers were first created simply by bolting a cockpit and engine block onto a cargo container, and the basic design has not changed much since then.

name: Hauler II (Turner Power)
description: This is a mid-sized freighter, designed for a balance between cargo capacity and defensive capability. Centuries ago, various Hauler models made up nearly half of the merchant vessels in service, but today the design is considered somewhat outdated.

name: Hauler III (Camouflaged Blaster)
description: Considered by many to be outdated, there are nonetheless many Haulers still in service due to their combination of comparatively low price and simple construction. This large version of the Hauler provides the best tradeoff between price and cargo space.

Missions: Midnight Market: Source of Coalition Smuggling 1

name: Hawk (Hai)
description: The Tarazed Hawk is an interceptor-class warship often used as an escort for freighters or in a planetary militia's patrol squadron. Hawks are fast enough to chase down most smaller ships, but are capable of mounting much more powerful weapons. They are also, of course, a favorite ship of pirate captains who have earned enough money to afford one.

description: The Headhunter is one of the newest ships designed by Lionheart: a versatile ship that can serve equally well for carrying cargo or for bounty hunting. As with many of Lionheart's designs, the hull is mostly composite fiber rather than metal, sacrificing armor strength for the sake of lighter weight and faster movement.

description: While Southbound Shipyards was developing the Nest, pirates near the galactic Core were modifying the cargo container of a salvaged Hauler with armor plating and shield emitters. This gave rise to Heavy Haulers. 	Although the thick armor plating is not impervious to scanners, it increases the time it takes to complete a scan of the Heavy Hauler's cargo container.

description: It wasn't long before demand for Heavy Hauler conversions lead to them being offered in shipyards. 	Although the thick armor plating is not impervious to scanners, it increases the time it takes to complete a scan of the Heavy Hauler's cargo containers.

name: Heavy Enforcer (Modified Blaster)
description: The Heavy Enforcers used by Dirtbelt pirates have their engines upgraded to offset the increased weight from adding heavy armor plating and extra shield emitters.

name: Heavy Enforcer (Turbo Blaster)
description: The Heavy Enforcers used by Dirtbelt pirates have their engines upgraded to offset the increased weight from adding heavy armor plating and extra shield emitters.

name: Heavy Shuttle (Spotlight)
description: The Heavy Shuttle is an updated shuttle model from Betelgeuse Shipyards, attempting to improve on their perennial classic by offering slightly better cargo and passenger capacity, at the expense of some speed and maneuverability.

name: Hogshead (Hijacked)
description: First manufactured by a forgotten shipyard many centuries ago, the Hogshead got its iconic name from the keg-like shape of its engine pods; its design was a hot topic of discussion for captains everywhere. The ship quickly became popular after first being released for consumer purchase and was commonly seen around various systems as the go-to "space bus" in human space. 	Unfortunately, only a few decades after introducing the ship, the shipyard went bankrupt and was never heard from again, leaving the Hogshead to history as their one-hit-wonder. In the current era, Hogsheads are much less common; they are considered "unsightly" compared to modern vessels such as the Star Queen, though a few places in human space still sell them.

name: Lance (Inferno Rocket)
description: The Lance is the primary fighter used by the Republic Navy. As with all fighters, it is weak by itself but very effective as part of a larger squadron. 	Fighters do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a fighter unless you have a ship in your fleet with a fighter bay.

Missions: Inferno Dragon: Inferno Rockets Appear Patch

name: Leviathan (Inferno Rockets)
description: The Betelgeuse Shipyards Leviathan has been in service since long before the Republic existed. Although considered today to be an outdated and clunky model, it is still a force to be reckoned with.

description: The Longsword is the result of the Saber's evolution among Dirtbelt pirates. The two additional gun ports on the ship's aft section are primarily used for the purpose of adding long range missiles.

description: After their Quicksilver light warship with its armament of Particle Cannons became a large success with convoy escorts and planetary defense forces, Megaparsec decided to develop a larger ship with similar maneuverability but heavier firepower. The Manta is the result: a ship with enough long-range firepower that fleeing from a battle with it is almost impossible.

name: Mega Freighter (Blaster)
description: The Syndicate's Mega Freighter is being marketed as a symbol for a new era of peace and prosperity in the post-war galaxy. Despite having double the cargo capacity of a Container Transport, the empty hull isn't much heavier unless it's carrying a full cargo load. In addition to the extra cargo space, this ship offers more flexibility for installing engines, weapons, and other outfits.

description: The semi-autonomous Mining Drone is used by the Syndicate alongside the Sunder mining vessel in a supportive role. Not being much more than a Mining Laser with an engine stuck to the rear of it, these drones do not last long in combat with other ships due to how fragile they are. 	Drones do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a drone unless you have a ship in your fleet with a drone bay.

description: For centuries, the Missileboat provided fleet admirals with a reliable artillery unit to support their front line vessels. The outdated systems interface on this ship have a higher crew requirement than the Rainmaker currently used by the Navy. Retired Missileboats are sometimes used by bounty hunters. 	This ship is exclusively offered as surplus inventory at the Geminus shipyards. If any are found for sale elsewhere, they were probably stolen.

name: Mule (Cudgel)
description: The Mule is as much of a hodgepodge as its looks suggest. The Lionheart ship designers combined a good deal of cargo space with a decent amount of weaponry and even a fighter bay, and ended up with a ship that is mostly used as a freighter but can also be used as a warship because of its heavy armament and shields. This hodgepodge nature has its downside, though, namely its high crew requirement and the fact that any dedicated ship of the same cost will do better in a specialized role than the Mule will.

name: Nest (Korath)
description: Southbound Shipyards quickly answered the Free Worlds' call for a carrier by building modified cargo containers for Haulers - adding a pair of Fighter docking arms, some additional outfit space, and even a couple of bunks. Access to the fighters from within the ship is so inconvenient that most pilots opt to just stay aboard their fighters when in flight. While the technical name for this ship is "Hauler I: Type F," Southbound assembly workers started calling the single container variant the "Nest" due to its complement of Finch Fighters, and the name stuck.

name: Osprey (Flamethrower)
description: After the Blackbird gained surprising popularity as an agile light warship despite its original design as a transport, Tarazed decided to produce a new design specifically intended as a warship. The Osprey was the result. Although it is considered overpriced by most savvy pilots, it is nevertheless a perennial favorite among those who have the extra cash to spend on it.

name: Outrider (Mark II)
description: The Navy Outrider interceptor was decommissioned with the rise of the Navy Carrier. But after the bombing of Geminus saw the Navy's ability to construct new capital ships hindered, the Republic has requested production of the Outrider be resumed to support their fleets. 	No Navy license is required to purchase this ship. It was previously made available to the public via the surplus ship sales program on Geminus.

name: Palisade (Hijacked)
description: The largest in a series of ships the Syndicate promoted as an affordable mass transport solution, Palisades are well protected by heavy hull plating and massive shield emitters.

name: Parapet (Spotlight)
description: Prioritizing quantity over quality, the Parapet formed the baseline for a series of ships the Syndicate promoted as an affordable mass transport solution.

name: Patrolboat (Turbo Blaster)
description: The Patrolboat is an old ship that previously performed the role which the modern Gunboat fulfills. The main reason the Navy replaced it was the lack of a turret mount. Some retired Patrolboats have found a new role as merchant escorts or bounty hunters. 	This ship is exclusively offered as surplus inventory at the Geminus shipyards. If any are found for sale elsewhere, they were probably stolen.

name: Pirate Drone (Modified)
description: Locals started making these in their backyard garages to satisfy a limited demand for unofficial combat drones. From a distance, it looks like someone duct taped an energy blaster to the side of a civilian science drone. Upon closer inspection, that assessment isn't too far off. Each drone has minor differences in construction materials and how the wires are routed. Few pirates actually use these, but the ones who do tend to buy them in bulk.

description: Locals started making these in their backyard garages to satisfy a limited demand for unofficial combat drones. name: Plunder (Kludge Blaster)
description: This ship is exclusively for sale to certain members of the Covert Mining Cartel. The Plunder is often mistaken for a modification of the Sunder currently sold by Syndicate Shipyards. But its lineage can be traced back to an older model that ceased production long ago. 	This confusion is exacerbated by the fact that the Plunder is indeed missing two of its original external cargo pods. However, these pods were located in the ship's undercarriage. Their removal allowed installation of a second drone bay. Unlike the second drone on the Syndicate's Sunder, this one faces forward, allowing it to quickly target the Plunder's prey.

name: Protector (Korath)
description: Voted the "ugliest ship in the sky" by Stars and Starships Magazine, the Protector is a typical example of brutally efficient Syndicate engineering. It is basically nothing more than eight turrets attached to a set of engines and crew's quarters, designed as a defense platform that can accompany merchant convoys.

name: Rainmaker (Inferno Rockets)
description: The Rainmaker is a missile boat built in the Republic Navy Yard. It comes equipped with four missile launchers and enough ammunition to destroy even ships many times its size. Because of their lack of maneuverability and relatively light shielding, Rainmakers serve best as long-range attack ships, and once their missile supply is spent, they are relatively useless in combat.

Missions: Inferno Dragon: Inferno Rockets Appear Patch

name: Rancor (Berserker)
description: The Rancor was originally a pirate vessel, and many were used in the early turf wars. The Alpha War was the beginning of the Rancor's decline as faster interceptors quickly outpaced and outmaneuvered this relatively slower ship. Today it is seen as a relic of the past, most noteworthy for influencing the design of the Fury.

name: Raven (Hai)
description: The Lionheart Raven is their most popular warship, an agile and elegant vessel that can nevertheless deal out and absorb considerable damage. Ravens are a favorite ship for bounty hunters, because they are more than a match for pirate interceptors and can be used by a skilled pilot to take down much larger ships as well.

Missions: Midnight Scar: Escort Stolen Vessel Job (Medium, North, Dangerous Path)

name: Roost (Korath)
description: Southbound Shipyards' new fighter-carrying containers have created a new use for what many considered an outdated design. Four external fighter bays make this the biggest civilian carrier on the market, if a little awkward as a warship. After Southbound assembly workers successfully named the "Nest," "Hauler II: Type F" was quickly dubbed the "Roost" to continue the theme.

name: Saber (Energy Cannons)
description: During the caravan era, a boom of economic development spread along the paths of more heavily armed and technologically advanced merchant fleets originating from the Deep, which resulted in ships such as the Saber being developed. This medium warship was one of the powerhouses of military might in its time, armed to the teeth with five gun ports in the nose and a dorsal turret mount to fire at anything not in front of it. As pirates began to join together in larger war bands in response to the more heavily defended merchants, the Saber became popular amongst them as well. Unlike other ships such as the Firebird, it slowly faded out as competition rose, but it is nevertheless cherished by those who still own one.

name: Scout (Bounty Hunter)
description: The Lionheart Scoutship was designed back in the days when the galaxy was more sparsely populated, and ships prospecting for habitable worlds or worlds rich in resources would often travel far away from any habitable ports where they would be able to refuel. Although the galaxy has now been much more thoroughly explored, the Scout remains a popular ship for long-distance travel.

name: Scrapper (Kludge Blaster)
description: Rather than being a specific model of ship, the Scrappers are instead a family of vessels cobbled together from the remains of broken shuttles. On the rare occasion that Republic authorities bother to recognize their existence, Scrappers are treated as almost-illegal, patent-infringing death traps. Despite this, these ships are where many captains (and pirates) from poorer backgrounds get their start.

description: Assembled from the burned out remains of wrecked freighters, Scroungers are how some pilots lacking the funds for better options begin their careers. Unfortunately, it is not uncommon for those facing financial desperation to turn to small scale acts of piracy, scavenging parts and cargo from the scraps leftover by larger raiders.

name: Shuttle (Spotlight)
description: Although Betelgeuse Shipyards produces other ship models as well, the majority of their profits come from sales of the Shuttle. This versatile ship serves equally well as a passenger transport or a cargo courier. It also happens to be the cheapest ship you can buy which is capable of hyperspace travel. 	Shuttles are not designed to withstand combat of any sort, but they are fast and maneuverable enough to get out of harm's way if attacked by a larger, slower ship. Although they are typically unarmed, they have enough space for one weapon, which is the origin of the popular phrase, "as useless as a blaster cannon on a shuttlecraft."

name: Shuttle Pod (Beam Laser)
description: Shuttle Pods were multipurpose light spacecraft that early exploration ships could carry in their fighter bays. While it is no longer in production, a multitude were made. Many are still in use throughout the galaxy despite their antiquated design. 	This particular Shuttle Pod configuration was the standard for transporting cargo and mining, but other configurations were used as well.

name: Skein (Catalytic)
description: This particular cockpit and engine set has found itself attached to three of Southbound Shipyards' new "Type F" Fighter-carrier pods. When the Free Worlds asked for a carrier, they did not expect one that would be able to carry almost as many fighters as the heaviest Navy warships. Given this fact, the Free Worlds have asked Southbound and Tarazed to restrict sale of the "Hauler III: Type F," "Skein" to its Militia only.

name: Sparrow (Afterburner)
description: Classified as an interceptor rather than a fighter because it has its own hyperdrive instead of needing to be carried inside a larger ship, the Tarazed Sparrow is the smallest and cheapest combat ship available. Because of its limited cargo and passenger space, the primary way for a Sparrow pilot to pay the bills is to upgrade the weapons and hunt pirates... or become one. In either case, it is a perilous way to earn a living. 	Government reports indicate that as many as two out of every three first-time ship buyers who choose to pilot a Sparrow lose their ship (and often, their life as well) within the first month of owning it. Pilots who have survived longer than that tend to swear by replacing the stock weapons with missile launchers.

name: Splinter (Inferno Rockets)
description: The Splinter is the largest warship produced by the Megaparsec shipyard. It is designed to be faster and more maneuverable than other ships of a similar size, in order to serve as a heavy escort ship or as part of a planetary defense and patrol fleet. Because of its intended use in merchant convoys, it also has a fair amount of cargo space.

name: Starbus (Meteor)
description: A major component to their Hauler freighters, Southbound's Starbus heavy shuttle can sacrifice mobility for increased armaments. 	When tensions arose between the Free Worlds and Republic, Southbound Shipyards brought the Starbus heavy shuttle back into production.

name: Star Barge (Smuggler)
description: The Syndicate CT-50 Star Barge is little more than a cluster of cargo containers with an engine at one end and a cockpit on the other. It is a slow and ugly ship, but it can carry enough cargo to earn its captain a steady income even in parts of the galaxy where it is hard to find passengers to carry or courier missions. 	Because they often carry valuable cargo, and because they are too slow to evade any attackers, Star Barges are a common target of pirates.

name: Star Explorer (Biohazard)
description: An ancient space exploration vessel from the early days of Hyperspace travel. Many claim the Star Explorer's design was heavily influenced by an old Earth science-fiction show as a marketing gimmick to entice young people to leave Sol and risk their lives exploring the vast unknown. Of the few that haven't been lost or destroyed, there are some still being used as actual science and exploration vessels. The rest are held in private collections by fans of antiquities.

name: Star King (Hijacked)
description: To celebrate a new era of peace in galactic history, Betelgeuse has brought the Star King back into production. An even larger luxury liner than their distinguished Star Queen, this vessel offers its passengers all of the high end amenities that wealthy travelers would expect from its smaller counterpart.

name: Star King (Hai Engines)
description: To celebrate a new era of peace in galactic history, Betelgeuse has brought the Star King back into production. An even larger luxury liner than their distinguished Star Queen, this vessel offers its passengers all of the high end amenities that wealthy travelers would expect from its smaller counterpart.

name: Star Queen (Advanced Research)
description: The Star Queen is a relic of a more peaceful period of galactic history: a luxury liner for high-end tourists. Every cabin has its own mini-bar, and the bathrooms have marble tile and showers that use actual hot water instead of chemicals or sonic vibrations. 	In support of environmental movements across the galaxy, all Star Queens come standard with an array of solar panels as their only source of energy as opposed to more popular power generators such as fuel cells or nuclear reactors that produce some amount of waste. Although some argue that this is a pointless move given the prevalence of clean fusion reactors, others have applauded it and cited the prohibitive costs of fusion reactors as a reason for using solar panels.

name: Starcadia (Prototype)
description: The technology on the Starcadia, while in line with other modern ships, is surprisingly mundane for a newly deployed ship from the Deep. This has caused some to speculate that the Deep has lost their edge in technological advancement. Alternatively, conspiracy theorists suggest that the Deep has been sitting on this design for some time and is only releasing it now in preparation for some threat unknown to the general public. Deep Security, for its part, has no comment on these rumors.

name: Stingray (Blaster)
description: With an empty hull weighing in at 180 tons, Megaparsec's Stingray is among the heaviest interceptors in the galaxy. They were once deployed by Syndicate security forces to escort freighter convoys and patrol trade routes, but the Syndicate has since replaced it with the more economically friendly Wasp. 	While some Stingrays can still be found protecting private merchant fleets or mining asteroids, many have found their way into the hands of pirates.

name: Striker Drone (Kludge Blaster)
description: Southbound Shipyards and Kraz Cybernetics hastily constructed this modified Surveillance Drone at the request of the Free Worlds. With barely enough weapon space to install anything larger than a small missile pod, the Striker drone can provide long distance fire support or aid in surprise tactical strikes.

name: Striker Drone (Meteor)
description: Southbound Shipyards and Kraz Cybernetics hastily constructed this modified Surveillance Drone at the request of the Free Worlds. With barely enough weapon space to install anything larger than a small missile pod, the Striker drone can provide long distance fire support or aid in surprise tactical strikes.

name: Sunder (Kludge Blaster)
description: The Sunder is a versatile mining platform equipped with dedicated prospecting equipment, most notably an undercarriage that supports two Mining Drones. The lineage of this ship can be traced back to the old asteroid mining ships used in the Sol Main Belt centuries ago; however, because of recent Syndicate efforts to further exploit the Core's mineral wealth, the newest model of the Sunder is now manufactured by Syndicated Shipyards.

name: Sunder (Smuggler)

The lineage of this ship can be traced back to the old asteroid mining ships used in the Sol Main Belt centuries ago; however, because of recent Syndicate efforts to further exploit the Core's mineral wealth, the newest model of the Sunder is now manufactured by Syndicated Shipyards.

name: Sunracer (Atomic Afterburner)
description: The ship of choice for most professional racing pilots. With a focus on maximizing speed, a Sunracer lacks space to spare for ubiquitous things like cargo, bunks, weapons, or a hyperdrive. 	These ships serve a unique niche and are exclusively sold at inflated prices on the planet they're named after. Although it's possible to squeeze one into a fighter bay, the prohibitive cost and minimal combat capability means there isn't a compelling reason to use them in that capacity.

name: Quicksilver (Korath)
description: The Megaparsec Quicksilver is a warship built around a single concept: to design the smallest and fastest ship capable of carrying two Particle Cannons. Because of its speed, reverse thrusters, and long weapons range, the Quicksilver can keep a safe distance from most targets and bombard them with particle bursts until they are destroyed. The focus on its weaponry left the Quicksilver with no room for even the most basic shield generator. Unfortunately the Headhunter eventually surpassed the original design intent, but the Quicksilver's better durability to cost ratio, and the existence of long-term contracts, has kept it in use with Syndicate security forces.

name: Quicksilver (Plasma)
description: The Megaparsec Quicksilver is a warship built around a single concept: to design the smallest and fastest ship capable of carrying two Particle Cannons. The focus on its weaponry left the Quicksilver with no room for even the most basic shield generator. Unfortunately the Headhunter eventually surpassed the original design intent, but the Quicksilver's better durability to cost ratio, and the existence of long-term contracts, has kept it in use with Syndicate security forces.

name: Vanguard (Inferno Rockets)
description: The Vanguard is Syndicated Systems' newest warship intended for the heavy anti-pirate defense market. Vanguards offer an unusual feature set, focusing on powerful fixed guns and featuring Syndicated Systems' latest automation technologies, resulting in an atypically low crew complement for a ship its size. Few capital ships will endure for long under the withering fusillade of gunfire that it can unleash against those in front of it.

name: War Hauler (Grudge Hammer)
description: A leftover from the Alpha Wars, War Hauler production stopped soon after the wars ended. Although the ship predates the Republic and is not manufactured by any modern shipyards, Parliament still passed a law prohibiting retail sales of this ship to ordinary civilians. This has made the few remaining War Haulers on the secondary market a highly sought after item amongst collectors of antique ships.

name: War Hauler (Memoria Alpha)
description: A leftover from the Alpha Wars, War Hauler production stopped soon after the wars ended. Although the ship predates the Republic and is not manufactured by any modern shipyards, Parliament still passed a law prohibiting retail sales of this ship to ordinary civilians. This has made the few remaining War Haulers on the secondary market a highly sought after item amongst collectors of antique ships.

name: Wasp (Kludge Blaster)
description: The Wasp is a medium-strength interceptor, designed by Syndicated Shipyards to serve as an escort for cargo vessels. Like most Syndicate ships, it is not particularly pretty, but it at least serves the purpose it was made for.

name: Wasp (Korath)
description: The Wasp is a medium-strength interceptor, designed by Syndicated Shipyards to serve as an escort for cargo vessels. Like most Syndicate ships, it is not particularly pretty, but it at least serves the purpose it was made for.

name: Wasp (Limit Break)
description: The Wasp is a medium-strength interceptor, designed by Syndicated Shipyards to serve as an escort for cargo vessels. Like most Syndicate ships, it is not particularly pretty, but it at least serves the purpose it was made for.

name: Wasp (Miner)
description: The Wasp is a medium-strength interceptor, designed by Syndicated Shipyards to serve as an escort for cargo vessels. Like most Syndicate ships, it is not particularly pretty, but it at least serves the purpose it was made for.

name: Bulk Freighter(hai)
description: The Bulk Freighter is the ultimate cargo ship, able to carry as much cargo as four ordinary Syndicate Freighters. Its on-board gearing allows it to service even the most primitive colonies, though dock workers in busier spaceports often joke that it would be simpler to carry smaller freighters within its gigantic holds. 	It is also an incredibly slow and difficult to maneuver ship, especially when loaded down with cargo. Some freight caravans use ordinary freighters instead of Bulk Freighters just to avoid the hassle of constantly having to wait for the Bulk Freighter to properly align itself for hyperspace travel.

name: Bulk Freighter(proton)
description: The Bulk Freighter is the ultimate cargo ship, able to carry as much cargo as four ordinary Syndicate Freighters. Its on-board gearing allows it to service even the most primitive colonies, though dock workers in busier spaceports often joke that it would be simpler to carry smaller freighters within its gigantic holds. 	It is also an incredibly slow and difficult to maneuver ship, especially when loaded down with cargo. Some freight caravans use ordinary freighters instead of Bulk Freighters just to avoid the hassle of constantly having to wait for the Bulk Freighter to properly align itself for hyperspace travel.

name: Mammoth (Insurgent Subjugation Gear)
description: This is a rare, extensively modified Behemoth used by some pirates and smugglers to transport large amounts of goods to systems both near and far. It is armed well enough to handle even a Navy Frigate if caught and fast enough to dash away from anything bigger.

name: Midnight Hauler VI (Hauler VI (prototype))
description: After a series of delays due to unfortunate events in the testing phase, the Hauler VI is finally available for purchase. There were rumors that an innovative new refrigerated storage system was being developed for it. But no such system is present in the final production model.

name: Missileboat (Rainmaker (Nuclear))
description: For centuries, the Missileboat provided fleet admirals with a reliable artillery unit to support their front line vessels. The outdated systems interface on this ship have a higher crew requirement than the Rainmaker currently used by the Navy. Retired Missileboats are sometimes used by bounty hunters. 	This ship is exclusively offered as surplus inventory at the Geminus shipyards. If any are found for sale elsewhere, they were probably stolen.

name: Missileboat (Rainmaker (Wanderer))
description: For centuries, the Missileboat provided fleet admirals with a reliable artillery unit to support their front line vessels. The outdated systems interface on this ship have a higher crew requirement than the Rainmaker currently used by the Navy. Retired Missileboats are sometimes used by bounty hunters. 	This ship is exclusively offered as surplus inventory at the Geminus shipyards. If any are found for sale elsewhere, they were probably stolen.

name: Scrapper (Turbo Blaster)
description: Rather than being a specific model of ship, the Scrappers are instead a family of vessels cobbled together from the remains of broken shuttles. On the rare occasion that Republic authorities bother to recognize their existence, Scrappers are treated as almost-illegal, patent-infringing death traps. Despite this, these ships are where many captains (and pirates) from poorer backgrounds get their start.

name: Scrounger (Turbo Blaster)
description: Assembled from the burned out remains of wrecked freighters, Scroungers are how some pilots lacking the funds for better options begin their careers. Unfortunately, it is not uncommon for those facing financial desperation to turn to small scale acts of piracy, scavenging parts and cargo from the scraps leftover by larger raiders.

name: Marauder Hauler (used)
description: Some Haulers are modified to reduce mass and drag by replacing the standard cargo container with something smaller. The previous owner of this Hauler took the standard Southbound Shipyards cargo container and cut it down to half its size.

name: Modified Argosy (used slaver)
description: The Argosy has long been a favorite ship for pirates, who have come up with many tricks for modifying them to be more suitable as warships.

Missions: Ship Sale: Modified Argosy (Negotiate Used Slaver)
Ship Sale: Modified Argosy (Screams of Madness)

Ship Sale: Modified Argosy (Screams of Insanity)

name: Marauder Arrow (Used)
description: This Arrow has had numerous modifications to its hull and internal systems to make more room for outfits - mostly with combat in mind. More shields, weapons, engines, outfit space, and a turret mount nearly push this ship into classification as a light warship, and definitely make it one of the heaviest interceptors you're likely to find.

name: Marauder Bounder (Used)
description: This Megaparsec Bounder has been modified from a courier-scout into a heavy escort interceptor - currently the largest in the class. A little extra outfit space, reinforced turret mounts, a new gun port in front of the pilot, and additional shield emitters grant this former transport both fearsome speed and armament.

description: This Tarazed Falcon has been heavily modified by some very dedicated craftsmen. The hull is riddled with extra shield emitters and expanded equipment bays, adding bulk and definitely voiding the warranty.

description: By the looks of the modification that have taken place, you suspect that this ship contains no original parts. With extra shield emitters, hull plating, and outfit space, this half-millennium-old design is finding new life with its heavy modification.

description: After the Syndicate released their Vanguard Heavy Warship, the Manta fell somewhat out of favor due to its lack of ability to mount any anti-missile turrets. The owner of this Manta rectified that, added some extra armor plating and shield emitters, and rearranged some of the internals to yield a little more outfit space. Dry tonnage alone keeps it from being classified as a heavy warship.

name: Marauder Raven (Used Engines)
description: Whoever modified this Raven has given more heft to an agile and elegant vessel. They've added a turret and some apparently-flimsy bulkheads over extra shield emitters and outfit space. If it weren't for the wings, the central hull would barely be recognizable as Lionheart's original under the patchwork that you expect has made an already deadly warship even more so.

name: Marauder Raven (Used Weapons)
description: Whoever modified this Raven has given more heft to an agile and elegant vessel. They've added a turret and some apparently-flimsy bulkheads over extra shield emitters and outfit space. If it weren't for the wings, the central hull would barely be recognizable as Lionheart's original under the patchwork that you expect has made an already deadly warship even more so.

name: Marauder Splinter (Used)
description: The Splinter is the largest warship produced by Megaparsec. Apparently someone wanted a bit more out of it, and has covered large sections of the hull with extra shield emitters and hull plating. Much of the stock cargo space has been converted to outfit, weapons, and engines space; coupled with a streamlining of existing internal systems, it makes this warship an agile and flexible war machine.

name: Missileboat (Used)
description: For centuries, the Missileboat provided fleet admirals with a reliable artillery unit to support their front line vessels. The outdated systems interface on this ship have a higher crew requirement than the Rainmaker currently used by the Navy. Retired Missileboats are sometimes used by bounty hunters. 	This ship is exclusively offered as surplus inventory at the Geminus shipyards. If any are found for sale elsewhere, they were probably stolen.

name: Patrolboat (Used)
description: The Patrolboat is an old ship that previously performed the role which the modern Gunboat fulfills. The main reason the Navy replaced it was the lack of a turret mount. Some retired Patrolboats have found a new role as merchant escorts or bounty hunters. 	This ship is exclusively offered as surplus inventory at the Geminus shipyards. If any are found for sale elsewhere, they were probably stolen.

name: Enforcer (Used)
description: Prior to the formation of the galactic Republic, many star systems were owned by "governing corporate entities," with the vast majority of space being inaccessible to private citizens. The only exception to this rule were the pirate bands, whose few ships were often stolen or rebellious corporate fleets of their own, yet who had an outsized impact on interstellar trade. The necessity for corporations to protect their operations meant that Enforcer was one of the most common vessels used for security and defense. The ship boasts an armament of four gun ports and a turreted mount, all packed on a small but defensible frame that was powerful for its time. 	Although no longer on the market, the Enforcer finds itself in the possession of a small number of merchants and, ironically, various groups of pirates, as an aging combat ship easily outperformed by other light warships or even interceptors of a similar size.

name: Modified Hogshead (Used)
description: Occasionally a pirate gets the idea in his head to adapt a ship not designed for warfare into a pirate vessel. Oftentimes the results weren't worth the effort. But some of these attempts manage to find a niche. A Modified Hogshead might appear under the command of bounty hunters or slavers who are likely to find a ship of this type useful in their line of work. 	Over half the passenger space on this Hogshead has been gutted to make room for more shield emitters and weapon systems to power the two additional gun ports. Thick armored plating has been welded over the passenger viewports and other vulnerable areas of the hull, making the ship heavier, but more durable.

name: Saber (Used)
description: During the caravan era, a boom of economic development spread along the paths of more heavily armed and technologically advanced merchant fleets originating from the Deep, which resulted in ships such as the Saber being developed. This medium warship was one of the powerhouses of military might in its time, armed to the teeth with five gun ports in the nose and a dorsal turret mount to fire at anything not in front of it. As pirates began to join together in larger war bands in response to the more heavily defended merchants, the Saber became popular amongst them as well. Unlike other ships such as the Firebird, it slowly faded out as competition rose, but it is nevertheless cherished by those who still own one.

name: Maeri'het (Cloak Armor 10)
description: The Maeri is the smallest Het ship created by the Builders' stations, a prime example of the genetic technology they once possessed. Its small size is compensated by the fact that it can hide its location from most types of sensors.

name: Maeri'het (Cloak Armor 20)
description: The Maeri is the smallest Het ship created by the Builders' stations, a prime example of the genetic technology they once possessed. Its small size is compensated by the fact that it can hide its location from most types of sensors.

name: Telis'het (Tactical Armor 10)
description: When the Builders designed the exoskeleton of the Telis, they came to the conclusion that it would have been impossible to make it resilient enough without compromising its agility and integrity. Instead, they focused heavily on improving their shield technology, giving the Telis many of their regenerators.

name: Telis'het (Tactical Armor 20)
description: When the Builders designed the exoskeleton of the Telis, they came to the conclusion that it would have been impossible to make it resilient enough without compromising its agility and integrity. name: Vareti'het (Durable Armor 20)
description: The Vareti was the strongest Het the Builders were able to complete, starting its mass production only a year before the beginning of the invasion. It relies on shield matrixes even more than its little brothers, using larger and more effective restorers that were developed alongside this project.

name: Rao'xeris (Key Stone)
description: A deep space mining vessel, the Rao'xeris was used to transport materials harvested in the northern reaches of Builder space to processing facilities in the southern region. When the Ka'het invasion began, many found that it could easily be converted into a capable warship. 	This ship is capable of diving into the harsh atmosphere of a gas giant.

name: Akfir A'awoj (Civilian)
description: The largest of Korath production vessels are derived from a common and ancient chassis. This utility vessel was converted into a warship early in the Korath civil war, but was quickly outclassed as the war intensified. It is named after a world that vanished from Korath space. Nir Akfir may still exist in the territory of another species. If it does, they probably refer to it by a different name.

name: Akfir A'awoj (Jump Limited)
description: The largest of Korath production vessels are derived from a common and ancient chassis. This utility vessel was converted into a warship early in the Korath civil war, but was quickly outclassed as the war intensified. It is named after a world that vanished from Korath space. Nir Akfir may still exist in the territory of another species. If it does, they probably refer to it by a different name.

Missions: Midnight Gorgon Heliarch Scouting - Peripheral Repair Hub

name: Far'itar (Jump)
description: Dedicated cargo transports are no longer necessary among the Korath Exiles. Rather than being scrapped, these ships have been repurposed to serve as mobile prisons. Criminals convicted of serious crimes are given a choice between a quick death via a microbot nutrigel processor or a life long assignment working on a Far'itar mining vessel. Prisoners who fail to perform their duties are still eligible for being tossed into the nutrigel processor. 	Operating these vessels can be lucrative for its captains, but the Korath authorities require them to activate their self destruct in the event of a mutiny or other event that could result in a prisoner escape. Whether or not they have the fortitude to fulfill this duty is put to the test whenever they engage in a boarding battle.

name: Kar'iyar (Ember)
description: The Kar'iyar is a lightly armed fighter transport ship. They primarily serve as escorts to supplement the fire power of larger Korath fleets.

name: Kar'iyar (Hyperdrive)
description: The Kar'iyar is a lightly armed fighter transport ship. They primarily serve as escorts to supplement the fire power of larger Korath fleets.

name: Sa'ifar (Firelight B)
description: A Sa'ifar houses a multitude of samples representing all natural life that could be salvaged from Korath worlds before they were ruined by the war. The crews operating these vessels fervently hope that their preservation efforts will one day contribute to the restoration of their ancient worlds.

name: Tch'esar (Damaged Jump Drive)
description: Tch'esar warships are no longer regarded as suitable for serious combat. However, they are still a capable platform for some of the Korath's heavier guns. The few which haven't been dismantled for scrap are utilized as patrol vessels and escorts to bolster the Korath home defense fleets.

name: Tch'esar (Hyperdrive)
description: Tch'esar warships are no longer regarded as suitable for serious combat. However, they are still a capable platform for some of the Korath's heavier guns. name: Tch'esar (Inferno-Trident)
description: Tch'esar warships are no longer regarded as suitable for serious combat. However, they are still a capable platform for some of the Korath's heavier guns. name: Tubfalet (flip)
description: The venerable Palavret has been a mainstay of the Korath raiding fleet, elements of its design dating back from before the era of shortages and desperation caused by a life in exile. Over forty generations of raiders have terrorized neighboring systems over the centuries. 	Constant warfare against surrounding aliens has been building pressure on Korath shipyards to recover some of their ancient prowess. Now, with a fresh industrial network to draw upon, the Korath have created a new raider to deal with modern targets, and the Tubfalet-Class Raiding Ship is named for an admiral who dealt with such enemies. Despite extensive injuries, he remained on his flag bridge during a Remnant attack, leading his squadron to victory.

name: Palavret (Korath Raider (Torpedo))
description: Successive raider designs have been the Korath warship of choice for plundering neighboring species for generations. Named for a celebrated captain who gave her life to ensure her crippled vessel made it back to port with its desperately needed cargo of stolen fish, the Palavret-Class Raiding Ship continues that grim tradition. 	Like all Exile ships, the Palavret has been adapted to house as many Korath as possible, as comfortably as possible, inflating its size beyond similarly armed warships. The spaceborne construction techniques employed allow for the ship's easy replacement, facilitating continued raids of nearby systems.

name: Palavret (Graviton Generator)
description: Successive raider designs have been the Korath warship of choice for plundering neighboring species for generations. name: Rano'erek (Civilian Scavenger)
description: After the Korath were exiled from their homelands, efficient resource collection became a priority. World-ships were too precious to risk on dangerous forays, and raiders lacked the cargo capacity. The Rano'erek-class Asteroid Dredging Ship, named for a respected civilian politician and economist, was born as an answer to this problem. 	Featuring an abundance of cargo and passenger space in addition to their formidable defensive armament, these bulky ships can also offer potent support for frontline Palavrets.

name: Rano'erek (Digger Scavenger)
description: After the Korath were exiled from their homelands, efficient resource collection became a priority. World-ships were too precious to risk on dangerous forays, and raiders lacked the cargo capacity. The Rano'erek-class Asteroid Dredging Ship, named for a respected civilian politician and economist, was born as an answer to this problem. 	Featuring an abundance of cargo and passenger space in addition to their formidable defensive armament, these bulky ships can also offer potent support for frontline Palavrets.

name: Kas'lor Ik 582 (Shock Strike)
description: The largest of Korath production vessels are derived from a common and ancient chassis. This relatively streamlined ship has been adapted to collect, refine, and transport hyperspace fuel. Its name refers to the delicious squids that lived in their gas giants, and especially Deka Dathnak.

name: Lor'nag Ik 590 (Shock Strike)
description: The largest of Korath production vessels are derived from a common and ancient chassis. This bulbous ship has been adapted to carry passengers between stations, or boarding parties into enemy strongholds. Its name is in memory of the Anatruskor, who invented the jump drive.

name: Ra'gru Ik 618 (Shock Strike)
description: The largest of Korath production vessels are derived from a common and ancient chassis. This deceptively spindly ship bristles with weapon emplacements for heavy raiding or defending other vessels. It is named after the Mereti's capital planet, where they made powerful warships out of modular components.

name: Ra'at Ik 621 (Shock Strike)
description: The largest of Korath production vessels are derived from a common and ancient chassis. This many-lobed ship has been adapted to carry fighters into large-scale raids, or mining craft into asteroid belts. Its radial arrangements of hangars allows fighters a maximum variety of safe approach patterns in combat. Sestor exiles, who had a great affinity for carriers and fighters, bequeathed this variant its name after their capital world.

name: Ikatila'ej (Thermal Obliterator)
description: For most of their manufacturing needs, the Korath rely, as even some terrestrial species do, upon their space station and asteroid infrastructure. Due to their spacebound existence, however, the acceptability for failure and downtime is slim to nonexistent. Should equipment aboard a station or a world-ship fail, the Ikatila'ej, named for an inventor famously proficient in a variety of fields, is called in to provide critical support. Its microbot-run factory produces basic living supplies, machine parts, and systems cores, and may assist in continuing stalled production. 	When not assisting with damaged ships and stations, or replenishing its own stores, the Ikatila'ej serves as an all-in-one recycling, mining, refining, and factory ship, using its microbot-powered grinder to rapidly turn asteroids and garbage into whatever products the civilian economic planning committees have determined to be in the most dire need.

name: Korsmanath A'awoj (Jump)
description: Korsmanath A'awojs, named in memory of the Korath home system, are massive nomadic habitats that hold much of their remaining population. True flying cities, they could be self-contained miniature worlds were it not for their heavy defensive armament and reliance on unsustainable materials, methods, and sources of power. 	Commission of new Korsmanath A'awojs is a resource-demanding, labor-intensive, painstaking process fraught with political intrigue. Navarchs fight with the civilian government for resources and yard time they would rather devote to building raiding and defensive fleets, civilians vie with each other for a place on the next ship, and planning committees argue with engineers over what methods and technologies to use. This leads to construction delays, corner-cutting, and over-militarization of the design at best, and violent feuds at worst.

name: Tek Far 71 - Lek (Jump)
description: The Kor Sestor war fleets rely heavily on small, powerful fighters and drones. This ship can carry up to ten of their drones, clamped to the exterior of its hull.

name: Tek Far 78 - Osk (Jump)
description: This carrier is equipped with docking clamps for up to nine fighters. When loaded with a full complement of the powerful Kor Sestor robotic fighters, it is a formidable opponent.

Missions: Midnight Wanderers: Sestor: Final Revenge B

name: Kar Ik Vot 349 (Secured)
description: The largest and deadliest of the Kor Sestor robotic warships, a single one of these battlecruisers can hold its own against an entire fleet of lesser ships.

name: Tek Far 71 - Lek (Secured)
description: The Kor Sestor war fleets rely heavily on small, powerful fighters and drones. This ship can carry up to ten of their drones, clamped to the exterior of its hull.

name: Tek Far 78 - Osk (Secured)
description: This carrier is equipped with docking clamps for up to nine fighters. When loaded with a full complement of the powerful Kor Sestor robotic fighters, it is a formidable opponent.

name: Tek Far 109 (Secured)
description: The TF109 is designed almost solely for the purpose of carrying a fleet of Kor Sestor fighters and drones. Without them to serve as a protective screen, the ship itself is relatively helpless.

name: Met Par Tek 53 (Secured)
description: Although this is the smallest Kor Sestor ship other than their fighters, it is larger than most human capital ships and considerably more powerful.

name: Model 8 (Secured)
description: The primary role of the smallest Kor Mereti drones is asteroid mining, but their lasers can also be employed as offensive weapons when necessary.

name: Model 16 (Secured)
description: The Kor Mereti war drones are constructed in a modular fashion, which allows them to grow over time as more materials become available. This one is recently built, and relatively small.

name: Model 32 (Secured)
description: The Kor Mereti built their drones to mimic life: capable of growth and development throughout their life cycles. Their automated factories can convert the drones from one configuration to another by adding or removing modules.

name: Model 64 (Secured)
description: This medium-sized drone serves the Kor Mereti fleets in much the same way as a human Frigate, but in terms of total firepower it is far beyond anything humans have constructed.

name: Model 128 (Secured)
description: As Kor Mereti drones continue to grow and develop into their final form, they split down the middle. At the same time, their shielding and weaponry continue to become stronger.

name: Model 256 (Secured)
description: There is a saying among the Quarg that every species which insists on studying war will eventually construct a weapon that outlives its creators. For the Korath, their war drones have very nearly done so.

name: Model 512 (Secured)
description: This is the final state in the evolution of a Kor Mereti war drone: a perfect ring, with incredibly powerful shields and turrets firing in every direction.

name: Pug Paragon Zibruka (Dual Seeker)
description: The Zibruka is the smallest Pug warship that you know of. It is a fearsome ship to face off against, especially in swarms. 	The Paragon variant of the Zibruka is intended for battles rated tier 2 and above. It has increased shielding and a reinforced hull. It also adds a turret mount and the additional space to use it.

description: The Enfolta is a medium-sized Pug warship. A single Enfolta can take on multiple smaller human ships. 	The Paragon variant of the Enfolta is intended for battles rated tier 2 and above. It has increased shielding and a reinforced hull. It also adds one more turret mount with the additional space to use it.

name: Pug Paragon Enfolta (Afterburner)
description: The Enfolta is a medium-sized Pug warship. A single Enfolta can take on multiple smaller human ships. 	The Paragon variant of the Enfolta is intended for battles rated tier 2 and above. It has increased shielding and a reinforced hull. It also adds one more turret mount with the additional space to use it.

name: Pug Paragon Maboro (Dual Seeker)
description: The Maboro appears to be the Pug's capital warship. It is a ship that only the strongest human capital ships are able to oppose. 	The Paragon variant of the Marboro is intended for battles rated tier 2 and above. It has increased shielding and a reinforced hull. It also adds two more turret mounts with the additional space to use them.

name: Pug Paragon Maboro (Ion Zapper)
description: The Maboro appears to be the Pug's capital warship. It is a ship that only the strongest human capital ships are able to oppose. 	The Paragon variant of the Marboro is intended for battles rated tier 2 and above. It has increased shielding and a reinforced hull. It also adds two more turret mounts with the additional space to use them.

name: Pug Paragon Maboro (Afterburner)
description: The Maboro appears to be the Pug's capital warship. It is a ship that only the strongest human capital ships are able to oppose. 	The Paragon variant of the Marboro is intended for battles rated tier 2 and above. It has increased shielding and a reinforced hull. It also adds two more turret mounts with the additional space to use them.

name: Pug Zibruka (Seeker)
description: The Zibruka is the smallest Pug warship that you know of. Despite having only two gun ports, its two advanced guns combined with its fast and efficient engine systems make it a fearsome ship to face off against, especially in swarms.

name: Pug Enfolta (Seeker)
description: The Enfolta is a medium-sized Pug warship. It has a formidable armament of advanced weaponry supported by powerful integrated energy generation. A single Enfolta can take on multiple smaller human ships.

name: Pug Enfolta (Zapper)
description: The Enfolta is a medium-sized Pug warship. It has a formidable armament of advanced weaponry supported by powerful integrated energy generation. A single Enfolta can take on multiple smaller human ships.

name: Pug Maboro (Seeker)
description: The Maboro appears to be the Pug's capital warship. Its shield matrix and hull are much weaker than human capital ships; however, it is able to regenerate its shields quickly. Its fearsome ordnance and advanced anti-missile defenses make it a ship that only the strongest human capital ships are able to oppose.

name: Pug Maboro (Zapper)
description: The Maboro appears to be the Pug's capital warship. Its shield matrix and hull are much weaker than human capital ships; however, it is able to regenerate its shields quickly. Its fearsome ordnance and advanced anti-missile defenses make it a ship that only the strongest human capital ships are able to oppose.

name: Tubfalet (Korath Raider (Chaos Hammer))
description: The venerable Palavret has been a mainstay of the Korath raiding fleet, elements of its design dating back from before the era of shortages and desperation caused by a life in exile. Over forty generations of raiders have terrorized neighboring systems over the centuries. 	Constant warfare against surrounding aliens has been building pressure on Korath shipyards to recover some of their ancient prowess. Now, with a fresh industrial network to draw upon, the Korath have created a new raider to deal with modern targets, and the Tubfalet-Class Raiding Ship is named for an admiral who dealt with such enemies. Despite extensive injuries, he remained on his flag bridge during a Remnant attack, leading his squadron to victory.

name: Armor Beetle (Arctic Dragon)
description: The Armor Beetle is a specialized Unfettered warship that has been optimized for combat at the expense of cargo space.

name: Prefect's Albatross (Albatross (Night Sword))
description: Once they left human space, it became apparent to the Remnant that they would need to build their own shipyards in order to defend themselves if they were discovered by the Alphas or any other unfriendly faction. Using new composite materials that they discovered, they built ships very different from anything seen in human space. The wings of this Albatross sweep back, giving it a sleek appearance. This marks it as a Prefect's Albatross, used on the rare occasion when a Prefect isn't riding on a cloaked ship.

name: Marauder Kestrel (Kestrel (Bloodwind))
description: Several years ago, while visiting a sweat lodge in search of spiritual renewal, Tarazed's chief ship designer fell into a trance and journeyed to a reality separate from our own. There he saw visions of a strange and elegant starship. Returning to his work with renewed purpose, he spent the next seven years making the Kestrel a reality.

name: Archon (Surveillance)
description: The Archon's presence is chilling. The "ship" has an almost organic appearance, but your ship's scanners give you no discernible information on its construction. What exactly it is you do not know, but you can feel that it is powerful.

Missions: Midnight Lost Orphan: Warden of Atrocity

name: Quarg Lindwyrm (Void Scraper)
description: The Quarg use the Lindwyrm as a heavy freighter, transporting in its ample cargo bays items both mundane and fantastical. In the days before the advent of modern cargo scanners revealed some measure of their contents, "the inside of a Quarg cargo hold" was slang used to describe any thing or place thought to be unknowable.

name: Quarg Drake (Explorer)
description: The Drake is an older ship used by the Quarg for reconnaissance and initial contact with emerging spacefaring species. A popular legend states that a Drake surveyed Sol while humans began to discover spaceflight.

name: Quarg Zilant (Void Scraper)
description: The Zilant is a fast assault ship used to harass and break enemy fleet formations or make quick strikes on targets behind enemy lines. In more peaceful regions of the galaxy, they are relegated to simple patrol and escort duties.

name: Quarg Wardragon (Scanner)
description: The Wardragon is used by the Quarg as a light warship. It is an older design within the Quarg's fleets, supposedly less intimidating compared to their larger warships and thus more suitable for dealings with particularly nascent species.

name: Quarg Wardragon (Void Scraper)
description: The Wardragon is used by the Quarg as a light warship. It is an older design within the Quarg's fleets, supposedly less intimidating compared to their larger warships and thus more suitable for dealings with particularly nascent species.

name: Quarg Wyvern (Explorer)
description: When humans first encountered Quarg vessels, with their bizarre, scaly-looking hulls and alien forms, the comparisons to dragons of different sorts rapidly proliferated. The Wyvern is a midsize warship of the Quarg, though the distinction between the Quarg warship classes is insignificant to all but the most powerful forces in the galaxy.

name: Quarg Skylark (Scanner)
description: The Skylark is used by the Quarg as a freighter and warship.

name: Quarg Skythrush (Scanner)
description: The Skythrush was designed to be used as a blockade runner. Over a thousand years have passed since anyone has dared lay siege to the Quarg, shifting their role towards cargo support and scouting.

name: Quarg Warkite (Scanner)
description: The Warkite is a nimble assault ship used by the Quarg to harass and break enemy fleet formations or make quick surgical strikes against targets behind enemy lines.

name: Kestrel (Modified Kestrel (Remnant Bays))
description: Several years ago, while visiting a sweat lodge in search of spiritual renewal, Tarazed's chief ship designer fell into a trance and journeyed to a reality separate from our own. There he saw visions of a strange and elegant starship. Returning to his work with renewed purpose, he spent the next seven years making the Kestrel a reality.

description: Several years ago, while visiting a sweat lodge in search of spiritual renewal, Tarazed's chief ship designer fell into a trance and journeyed to a reality separate from our own. There he saw visions of a strange and elegant starship. Returning to his work with renewed purpose, he spent the next seven years making the Kestrel a reality.

name: Kestrel (Modified Kestrel (Remnant Shields))
description: Several years ago, while visiting a sweat lodge in search of spiritual renewal, Tarazed's chief ship designer fell into a trance and journeyed to a reality separate from our own. There he saw visions of a strange and elegant starship. Returning to his work with renewed purpose, he spent the next seven years making the Kestrel a reality.

name: Kestrel (Modified Kestrel (Remnant Weapons))
