There he saw visions of a strange and elegant starship. Returning to his work with renewed purpose, he spent the next seven years making the Kestrel a reality.

name: Modified Bactrian (Remnant)
description: The owner of this Bactrian has replaced some of it's armored hull plating with additional shield emitters. This has made the ship lighter and faster. An additional fighter can be carried on a landing pad on the roof.

name: Raptor Prototype (unarmed)
description: The Raptor Prototype is the result of experiments utilizing biological specimens collected from across the galaxy and beyond. As a result, its design is a bit different from other Remnant ships.

name: Shrike (Thrasher)
description: The Shrike is a nimble gunship that is usually outfitted to provide long distance fire support. Remnant captains embarking on solo missions might opt for a more aggressive outfit selection instead.

name: Shrike (disarmed)
description: The Shrike is a nimble gunship that is usually outfitted to provide long distance fire support. Remnant captains embarking on solo missions might opt for a more aggressive outfit selection instead.

Missions: Grave Digger: Graveyard Robbery Job A
Grave Digger: Graveyard Robbery Job B
Grave Digger: Graveyard Robbery Job C

name: Shrike (Gravedigger)
description: The Shrike is a nimble gunship that is usually outfitted to provide long distance fire support. Remnant captains embarking on solo missions might opt for a more aggressive outfit selection instead.

name: Wren (Research)
description: Thanks to its cloaking technology, the Wren provides discreet transportation to remote Remnant research outposts. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Wren's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

name: Wren (Gravedigger)
description: Thanks to its cloaking technology, the Wren provides discreet transportation to remote Remnant research outposts. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Wren's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

name: Shadow Falcon (B)
description: This Tarazed Falcon has had some modifications done. The hull has no enhancements to shields, engines, or weapons. But it does have built in radar jamming, scan interference, and enhanced heat dissipation. Whoever designed this seemed to be intent on creating a stealth warship.

name: Shadow Hawk (B)
description: This Tarazed Hawk has had some modifications done. The hull has no enhancements to shields, engines, or weapons. But it does have built in radar jamming, scan interference, and enhanced heat dissipation. Whoever designed this seemed to be intent on creating a stealth warship.

name: Shadow Osprey (B)
description: This Tarazed Osprey has had some modifications done. The hull has no enhancements to shields, engines, or weapons. But it does have built in radar jamming, scan interference, and enhanced heat dissipation. Whoever designed this seemed to be intent on creating a stealth interceptor.

name: Shadow Raven (B)
description: This Lionheart Raven has had some modifications done. The hull has no enhancements to shields, engines, or weapons. But it does have built in radar jamming, scan interference, and enhanced heat dissipation. Whoever designed this seemed to be intent on creating a stealth warship.

name: Falcon (Remnant B)
description: The Tarazed Falcon is one of the most powerful warships that civilians can buy, a sleeker and more maneuverable alternative to the venerable and somewhat outdated Leviathan.

name: Falcon (Remnant)
description: The Tarazed Falcon is one of the most powerful warships that civilians can buy, a sleeker and more maneuverable alternative to the venerable and somewhat outdated Leviathan.

name: Hawk (Remnant B)
description: The Tarazed Hawk is an interceptor-class warship often used as an escort for freighters or in a planetary militia's patrol squadron. Hawks are fast enough to chase down most smaller ships, but are capable of mounting much more powerful weapons. They are also, of course, a favorite ship of pirate captains who have earned enough money to afford one.

name: Hawk (Remnant)
description: The Tarazed Hawk is an interceptor-class warship often used as an escort for freighters or in a planetary militia's patrol squadron. Hawks are fast enough to chase down most smaller ships, but are capable of mounting much more powerful weapons. They are also, of course, a favorite ship of pirate captains who have earned enough money to afford one.

name: Osprey (Remnant B)
description: After the Blackbird gained surprising popularity as an agile light warship despite its original design as a transport, Tarazed decided to produce a new design specifically intended as a warship. The Osprey was the result. Although it is considered overpriced by most savvy pilots, it is nevertheless a perennial favorite among those who have the extra cash to spend on it.

name: Osprey (Remnant)
description: After the Blackbird gained surprising popularity as an agile light warship despite its original design as a transport, Tarazed decided to produce a new design specifically intended as a warship. The Osprey was the result. Although it is considered overpriced by most savvy pilots, it is nevertheless a perennial favorite among those who have the extra cash to spend on it.

name: Raven (Remnant B)
description: The Lionheart Raven is their most popular warship, an agile and elegant vessel that can nevertheless deal out and absorb considerable damage. Ravens are a favorite ship for bounty hunters, because they are more than a match for pirate interceptors and can be used by a skilled pilot to take down much larger ships as well.

name: Raven (Remnant)
description: The Lionheart Raven is their most popular warship, an agile and elegant vessel that can nevertheless deal out and absorb considerable damage. Ravens are a favorite ship for bounty hunters, because they are more than a match for pirate interceptors and can be used by a skilled pilot to take down much larger ships as well.

name: Albatross (Gravedigger)
description: Once they left human space, it became apparent to the Remnant that they would need to build their own shipyards in order to defend themselves if they were discovered by the Alphas or any other unfriendly faction. Using new composite materials that they discovered, they built ships very different from anything seen in human space.

name: Starling (Gravedigger)
description: One of the strangest and most useful things that the Remnant have discovered in the abandoned worlds of the Ember Waste is alien cloaking technology. They have designed their scout ships to cloak when needed in order to explore alien territory without being seen... or to escape in a hurry after stealing something particularly valuable from those aliens. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Starling's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

name: Starling (Research)
description: One of the strangest and most useful things that the Remnant have discovered in the abandoned worlds of the Ember Waste is alien cloaking technology. They have designed their scout ships to cloak when needed in order to explore alien territory without being seen... or to escape in a hurry after stealing something particularly valuable from those aliens. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Starling's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

name: Gull (Gravedigger)
description: While the Starling was the first successful ship to use technology derived from alien ruins, the Gull is the most recent. The current increase in Korath activities has caused foraging for resources to become a risky endeavor, forcing the Remnant to develop a specialized freighter for hauling cargo from remote sites without revealing their presence to hostile aliens. 	The Gull has quickly proven its worth, allowing the Starling fleets to return to riskier activities while the Gull takes over the hazardous convoy routes. It is now the primary element of the Remnant's extra-territorial resource operations. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Gull's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

name: Gull (Research)
description: While the Starling was the first successful ship to use technology derived from alien ruins, the Gull is the most recent. The current increase in Korath activities has caused foraging for resources to become a risky endeavor, forcing the Remnant to develop a specialized freighter for hauling cargo from remote sites without revealing their presence to hostile aliens. 	The Gull has quickly proven its worth, allowing the Starling fleets to return to riskier activities while the Gull takes over the hazardous convoy routes. It is now the primary element of the Remnant's extra-territorial resource operations. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Gull's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

name: Penguin (Gravedigger)
description: Inspired by close contact with void sprites and the reinvigoration of xenobiology, the Penguin is a prototype designed to serve the dual roles of testing new starship technology while exploring hostile planets throughout the Ember Waste. One of this ship's distinguishing features is that it is truly alive and has a mind that approaches self-awareness. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Penguin's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

name: Pelican (disarmed)
description: The Pelican is closely based on the original semi-organic materials the Remnant found in the Ember Waste. While they are not technically alive, their internal mechanisms and method of construction bear such a close resemblance to living organisms that they are often considered as such. One key feature of this living resemblance is the innate sensory capabilities they possess, akin to sensor suites less advanced ships require for detailed scanning. These ships serve as the backbone of Remnant bulk salvage efforts, as well as their fast-logistics distribution network.

Missions: Grave Digger: Graveyard Robbery Job A
Grave Digger: Graveyard Robbery Job B
Grave Digger: Graveyard Robbery Job C

name: Pelican (Gravedigger)
description: The Pelican is closely based on the original semi-organic materials the Remnant found in the Ember Waste. While they are not technically alive, their internal mechanisms and method of construction bear such a close resemblance to living organisms that they are often considered as such. One key feature of this living resemblance is the innate sensory capabilities they possess, akin to sensor suites less advanced ships require for detailed scanning. These ships serve as the backbone of Remnant bulk salvage efforts, as well as their fast-logistics distribution network.

name: Ibis (disarmed)
description: The Ibis is a multipurpose carrier that serves as a mobile mining platform, an escort ship for Pelican fleets, and a combat platform using Terns for screening. It is capable of fielding a single wing of support craft, consisting of two Petrels and four Terns. 	The Ibis was designed shortly after the Albatross as a means of extending the range of resource collection efforts after the Remnant realized that an Alpha invasion might not be as imminent as originally believed.

Missions: Grave Digger: Graveyard Robbery Job A
Grave Digger: Graveyard Robbery Job B
Grave Digger: Graveyard Robbery Job C

name: Ibis (Gravedigger)
description: The Ibis is a multipurpose carrier that serves as a mobile mining platform, an escort ship for Pelican fleets, and a combat platform using Terns for screening. It is capable of fielding a single wing of support craft, consisting of two Petrels and four Terns. 	The Ibis was designed shortly after the Albatross as a means of extending the range of resource collection efforts after the Remnant realized that an Alpha invasion might not be as imminent as originally believed.

name: Modified Argosy (Sheragi Counterfeit)
description: The Argosy has long been a favorite ship for pirates, who have come up with many tricks for modifying them to be more suitable as warships.

name: Marauder Falcon (Sheragi Counterfeit)
description: This Tarazed Falcon has been heavily modified by some very dedicated craftsmen. The hull is riddled with extra shield emitters and expanded equipment bays, adding bulk and definitely voiding the warranty.

name: Marauder Firebird (Sheragi Counterfeit)
description: By the looks of the modification that have taken place, you suspect that this ship contains no original parts. With extra shield emitters, hull plating, and outfit space, this half-millennium-old design is finding new life with its heavy modification.

name: Marauder Leviathan (Sheragi Counterfeit)
description: The Betelgeuse Shipyards Leviathan has been in service for a long time, and captains have had some very interesting ideas about how to modify them for optimum performance. This one has had the hull surface completely stripped and replaced, with the new layout containing more shield projectors per square meter.

name: Marauder Manta (Sheragi Counterfeit)
description: After the Syndicate released their Vanguard Heavy Warship, the Manta fell somewhat out of favor due to its lack of ability to mount any anti-missile turrets. The owner of this Manta rectified that, added some extra armor plating and shield emitters, and rearranged some of the internals to yield a little more outfit space. Dry tonnage alone keeps it from being classified as a heavy warship.

name: Marauder Quicksilver (Sheragi Counterfeit)
description: This Megaparsec Quicksilver is a bit of a hotrod, being a little faster, with extra shield projectors, hull plating, and an extra bunk. This aftermarket model also features a turret mount, perhaps in an answer to Lionheart's Headhunter. The shop that built this ship is sure to see more customers.

name: Marauder Raven (Sheragi Counterfeit)
description: Whoever modified this Raven has given more heft to an agile and elegant vessel. They've added a turret and some apparently-flimsy bulkheads over extra shield emitters and outfit space. If it weren't for the wings, the central hull would barely be recognizable as Lionheart's original under the patchwork that you expect has made an already deadly warship even more so.

name: Marauder Splinter (Sheragi Counterfeit)
description: The Splinter is the largest warship produced by Megaparsec. Apparently someone wanted a bit more out of it, and has covered large sections of the hull with extra shield emitters and hull plating. Much of the stock cargo space has been converted to outfit, weapons, and engines space; coupled with a streamlining of existing internal systems, it makes this warship an agile and flexible war machine.

name: Marauder Vanguard (Sheragi Counterfeit)
description: This Syndicate Shipyards Vanguard has been customized with additional armored plating as well as upgraded shielding to accommodate the latest experimental technologies.

name: Diamondback (Lasers)
description: Diamondbacks have been in use since explorers first settled the galactic rim. They've fought in various wars throughout the centuries and few survive to this day. Those that have are so extensively customized that no two are alike. Some southern legends claim the Diamondbacks were wrecked spacecraft created by extinct aliens and salvaged by early explorers such as Han Sizer. There is no evidence to support these claims and no reputable scholar takes them seriously.

description: Diamondbacks have been in use since explorers first settled the galactic rim. They've fought in various wars throughout the centuries and few survive to this day. name: Kvelqde-Sosrue (Stealth)
description: This Kvelqde variant is designed to optimize the use of "Myiara" Fire Spouts. There are whispers that those who pilot such vessels are patron servants of a secret House that manipulates events from the shadows. Within the respectable echelons of Successor society such tales are dismissed as mere superstition, no more real than the monster from a children's poem. 	This ship can install Predecessor Multimode Armor materials.

name: Kvelqde-Sosrue (Tundra)
description: This Kvelqde variant is designed to optimize the use of "Myiara" Fire Spouts. There are whispers that those who pilot such vessels are patron servants of a secret House that manipulates events from the shadows. Within the respectable echelons of Successor society such tales are dismissed as mere superstition, no more real than the monster from a children's poem. 	This ship can install Predecessor Multimode Armor materials.

description: This Kvelqde variant is designed to optimize the use of "Myiara" Fire Spouts. Within the respectable echelons of Successor society such tales are dismissed as mere superstition, no more real than the monster from a children's poem. 	This ship can install Predecessor Multimode Armor materials.

name: Stolsaqra-Sosrue (Plate)
description: This variant Stolsaqra is a test model developed by Shadow House. Like the Kvelqde-Sosrue, it is designed to accomodate a pair of "Myiara" Fire Spouts into its arsenal. 	This ship can install Predecessor Multimode Armor materials.

name: Stolsaqra-Sosrue (Stealth)
description: This variant Stolsaqra is a test model developed by Shadow House. Like the Kvelqde-Sosrue, it is designed to accomodate a pair of "Myiara" Fire Spouts into its arsenal. 	This ship can install Predecessor Multimode Armor materials.

name: Kvelqde-Kiitaa (OC)
description: Many of the features of this Kvelqde have been scaled back, allowing it to be carried within a fighter bay without compromising its durability.

name: Spring Leaf (Jump)
description: This ancient Wanderer transport was among the ships originally in use when they first arrived in our galaxy. Now, obsolete, the Spring Leaf is best suited for display in a museum.

name: Winter Leaf (Jump)
description: With the Unfettered Hai behind them and the unknown beyond the Eye ahead, Wanderer shipyards are prepared to phase out production of the Summer Leaf in favor of this ancient alternative.

name: Tornado (Jump)
description: An ancient Wanderer warship, the Tornado was likely used to fulfill the same role as the modern Tempest. There are many similarities between the two ships.

name: Tsunami (Jump)
description: The Tsunami is a mobile fortress. Among the ancient Wanderer vessels, it was their closest equivalent to a city-ship.

name: Deep River Pirate 0
description: The Deep River is a modular freighter, designed to carry cargo in detachable pods.

name: Deep River Pirate 1
description: The Deep River is a modular freighter, designed to carry cargo in detachable pods.

name: Derecho (Advanced Cooling)
description: Like a latent gene waiting to express itself, the knowledge of how to wage war in space has been passed down through hundreds of generations of Wanderers. Based on one of their ancient designs, the Derecho has a mixture of agility and raw firepower that is well-suited to their present, rapidly changing situation.

name: Hurricane (Advanced Cooling)
description: The Hurricane is not built for speed, elegance, or versatility, but for one simple purpose: to be durable enough to serve as a rearguard, absorbing an enemy's attacks for long enough to allow the fleet it is guarding to escape.

name: Flycatcher (Laser)
description: Flycatcher drones are used by the Wanderers to keep their star systems safe. Rather than carrying weapons of its own, a stock Flycatcher just carries an anti-missile turret for defending other ships. 	Drones do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a drone unless you have a ship in your fleet with a drone bay.

name: Autumn Leaf (Hai Crew)
description: The Autumn Leaf is based on the Wanderer Summer Leaf light warship, but slightly larger and better equipped for combat. Unlike Wanderer peacetime ships, it is equipped with a turret mount in addition to the ports for fixed guns.

name: Strong Wind (Hai Crew)
description: The Strong Wind was originally designed for science and exploration, but recently the Wanderers have modified the design to allow it to be used as a warship.

name: Tempest (Hai Crew)
description: In response to the Unfettered invasion, the Wanderers have begun digging up ancient warship designs from their archives and putting them back into production. The Tempest is the first purpose-built warship they have manufactured in tens of thousands of years.

name: Derecho (Hai Crew)
description: Like a latent gene waiting to express itself, the knowledge of how to wage war in space has been passed down through hundreds of generations of Wanderers. Based on one of their ancient designs, the Derecho has a mixture of agility and raw firepower that is well-suited to their present, rapidly changing situation.

name: Hurricane (Hai Crew)
description: The Hurricane is not built for speed, elegance, or versatility, but for one simple purpose: to be durable enough to serve as a rearguard, absorbing an enemy's attacks for long enough to allow the fleet it is guarding to escape.

name: Summer Leaf (Missile)
description: The Summer Leaf is mostly used by the Wanderers as a light transport, but it is also quite effective in combat.

name: Autumn Leaf (Missile)
description: The Autumn Leaf is based on the Wanderer Summer Leaf light warship, but slightly larger and better equipped for combat. Unlike Wanderer peacetime ships, it is equipped with a turret mount in addition to the ports for fixed guns.

name: Strong Wind (Shields)
description: The Strong Wind was originally designed for science and exploration, but recently the Wanderers have modified the design to allow it to be used as a warship.

name: Deep River Carrier (Full)
description: This is a Deep River freighter that has had some of its cargo container mounts modified to carry drones.

name: Deep River Carrier (Half)
